data.title: Irish Government Urged to Halt Encryption Legislation Amid Privacy Concerns
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a privacy coalition has called on the Irish government to cease efforts on a pre-draft bill that would allow law enforcement access to encrypted messages. Ryan Polk, representing the Global Encryption Coalition (GEC), expressed concerns in an open letter about the proposed legislation by Minister for Justice Jim O’Callaghan. Polk warned that such measures could escalate cybercrime and deter businesses from establishing operations in Ireland. He emphasized that undermining encryption could threaten privacy and security globally, particularly as Ireland hosts the EU headquarters of major tech companies like Apple and Meta.

The Communications, Interception and Lawful Access Bill is still in its preliminary stages, with drafting anticipated to begin in the coming months. The GEC is advocating for the abandonment of this legislation. This appeal follows Germany's recent opposition to the EU Chat Control bill, which aims to enable authorities to scan messages before encryption. The coalition has also urged Ireland to retract its support for the Chat Control proposal, citing privacy and national security concerns.

Polk argues that weakening encryption would expose individuals and businesses to increased cybercrime risks, such as fraud and identity theft. He contends that it is a misconception to believe encryption can be weakened solely for legitimate purposes without creating vulnerabilities for malicious actors. Additionally, he highlighted that such actions could make sensitive data more susceptible to foreign cyberattacks, compromising national security. Ireland, home to many global tech giants due to its strategic location and favorable tax rates, could face significant challenges if the bill becomes law. Companies offering end-to-end encryption might be forced to weaken their security measures or exit the Irish market, resulting in diminished security and privacy for Irish citizens and institutions.

The GEC is also urging the Irish government to reconsider its stance on the EU’s Chat Control law, which mandates message screening before encryption. Germany's largest political party has recently opposed this measure, complicating its progress. The coalition believes Ireland could significantly influence the future of the Chat Control proposal when it assumes the EU Council’s presidency in July 2026. The GEC stresses the importance of not underestimating the severe consequences of weakening encryption, as the privacy and security of European citizens and beyond are at stake.

data.title: Monero Enhances Privacy with Major Upgrade
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Monero, a privacy-focused blockchain, has released a significant upgrade aimed at bolstering user protection against 'spy nodes.' Monero operates on a decentralized peer-to-peer (P2P) network, where nodes (computers) connect directly to share and verify transactions and blocks.

Monero's privacy features are supported by several key technologies. Each transaction utilizes a unique stealth address to ensure the recipient's real address remains undisclosed. Ring signatures blend the sender's transaction with other virtual transactions, making it difficult to identify the actual sender of funds. Additionally, ring confidential transactions conceal the transaction amount, further enhancing privacy.

data.title: OKX to Launch Pre-Market Trading for MON (Monad) and MET (Meteora)
data.description: According to an official announcement, OKX will launch pre-market trading for MON (Monad) and MET (Meteora). The specific schedule is as follows: MON/USDT pre-market trading will open at 4:00 PM (UTC+8) on October 10, 2025; MET/USDT pre-market trading will open at 4:15 PM (UTC+8) on October 10, 2025. Monad is an Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)-compatible Layer 1 blockchain designed to address the scalability limitations of existing networks. Meteora is a decentralized exchange dedicated to providing secure, sustainable, and composable liquidity infrastructure for the Solana ecosystem and the broader DeFi space.

data.title: Ceffu Secures Full VASP Custody License from Dubai’s VARA, Expanding Institutional Crypto Services in the Middle East
data.description: Key Takeaways:Ceffu Custody FZE has received a full Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) license from the Dubai Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) to offer custody services.The approval allows Ceffu to deliver regulated institutional-grade crypto custody to clients in and from Dubai.The platform leverages multi-party computation (MPC), multi-approval schemes (MAS), and transaction approval policy (TAP) to ensure top-tier security.Ceffu plans to expand its offering to include off-exchange settlement, hot wallet solutions, and institutional staking.The license positions Ceffu as a trusted custody provider in Dubai’s rapidly growing virtual asset ecosystem.Ceffu has officially secured a full Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) operating license from the Dubai Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA), marking a major milestone in its regional expansion.The license authorizes Ceffu Custody FZE (“Ceffu Dubai”) to provide comprehensive digital asset custody services to institutional clients both in and from Dubai.This development strengthens Ceffu’s role as a leading institutional crypto custody provider in the Middle East and underscores Dubai’s commitment to fostering a secure, transparent digital asset environment.“This full operating license from VARA reflects both the robustness of Dubai’s regulatory environment and Ceffu’s commitment to regulatory excellence,” said Ian Loh, CEO of Ceffu.“With the Middle East rapidly emerging as a global hub for cryptocurrency and blockchain, this license reinforces our ability to provide secure and compliant custody infrastructure for institutional clients.”Institutional-Grade Security and ComplianceCeffu’s custody infrastructure integrates multi-party computation (MPC) technology with customizable multi-approval schemes (MAS), transaction approval policy (TAP), and secure cold storage systems — ensuring both flexibility and resilience for institutional operations.These systems are designed to meet the stringent custodial needs of institutional investors, balancing security, accessibility, and compliance under VARA’s regulatory oversight.Ceffu Custody FZE also plans to expand its licensed activities in collaboration with VARA, tailoring solutions for asset managers, exchanges, and large-scale investors seeking regulated digital asset custody.

data.title: Ark Invest Reduces Stake in Brera Holdings
data.description: According to Foresight News, Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, has recently sold 44,859 shares of Brera Holdings. The transaction is valued at approximately $840,000. This move reflects Ark Invest's ongoing portfolio adjustments.

data.title: The founder of GMGN ranked as the top two holdings and promised not to sell, causing the SCI6900 to surge 400% in a short period of time.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the new BSC coin "SCI 6900" surged in popularity within two hours of its launch, likely due to the holdings and comments of GMGN.AI founder Haze. The price of the coin surged by over 440%, surpassing the meme coin "Xiuxian" in six-hour trading volume, reaching a market capitalization of $21 million and currently trading at $0.021. The holdings list shows that GMGN.ai co-founder Haze gmgn.ai is the second-largest holder, currently holding approximately $500,000, or 2.26% of the total. The top 10 holdings account for 14.36%, with an average floating profit exceeding 23 times, indicating a high concentration of holdings. Haze also stated in a post, "I am buying only for product testing and would not sell even if it were worth millions. GMGN never sells, only provides liquidity, and calls for a return to the original joy of memecoin—player versus player (PvE), not player versus player (PvP)."

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $198 million yesterday, marking the ninth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $198 million yesterday (October 9th, EST). The Blackrock IBIT ETF saw the largest single-day net inflow yesterday, with a net inflow of $255 million, bringing IBIT's total net inflow to $65.19 billion. The next largest single-day net inflow was the Bitwise BITB ETF, with a net inflow of $6.58 million, bringing BITB's total net inflow to $2.52 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $164.79 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to the total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.83%, and cumulative net inflows to $62.77 billion.

data.title: Binance to Adjust Leverage and Margin Tiers for Multiple Contracts
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance Futures will adjust the leverage and margin tiers of several USD-M perpetual contracts at 2:30 PM (Beijing Time) on October 11, 2025, and 2:30 PM (Beijing Time) on October 14, 2025. Affected contracts include COAIUSDT, TAKEUSDT, NAORISUSDT, A2ZUSDT, ARIAUSDT, TSTUSDT, GUSDT, PUNDIXUSDT, 1000CATUSDT, HYPERUSDT, PROMPTUSDT, and ZECUSDT. (This newsletter was generated with AI assistance.)
